title: Aurora Cannabis Q3 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Aurora Cannabis reported Q3 2026 net revenue of $94.2 million, representing 7% year-over-year growth. Global medical cannabis segment achieved record performance with $76.2 million in revenue, up 12%, while international medical cannabis grew 17%. Management noted more than half of total net revenue was generated outside of Canada, signaling successful geographic diversification. Profitability metrics: Adjusted gross margin expanded 100 basis points to 62%, with medical cannabis segment maintaining robust 69% margin. Adjusted EBITDA reached $18.5 million, adjusted net income held steady at $7.2 million compared to $7.4 million in prior year quarter. Free cash flow totaled $15.5 million. Strategic restructuring: Aurora announced exit from select lower-margin Canadian consumer cannabis markets to reallocate resources toward higher-margin international medical operations. Company will divest controlling stake in Bevo plant propagation business, which saw adjusted gross margin decline to 16% from 40% due to operational challenges including $1.1 million inventory write-off. Capital deployment: Company established $100 million at-the-market equity program, with proceeds designated for strategic and accretive purposes only, including cultivation expansion and potential M&A. Aurora maintained strong balance sheet with $154 million cash and zero cannabis business-related debt. Forward guidance: Management projects full-year global medical cannabis revenue between $269-$281 million, with consolidated adjusted EBITDA guidance of $52-$57 million, representing 5-10% annual growth. Company emphasized competitive advantage: approximately 90% of manufacturing capacity operates from European and Australian GMP-certified facilities.
